findings.rationale: No production workload billing impact. Changes are dev-only tooling in a governance submodule. No showback/chargeback or unit cost metric changes. CI compute overhead is single-digit seconds per run.
No new cloud resources to tag or budget. pytest-html is dev-only and does not enter production dependency trees. No commitment coverage or reservation implications.

Lowering Python floor preserves existing cached CI images. Self-contained HTML report adds 30-100 KB disk I/O per test run — negligible. Consider gating report generation behind CI-only flag for local development convenience.
No IaC modifications. Zero incremental cloud resource cost across all environments. No egress, data transfer, or licensing implications.

Token budget impact is minimal. Generated HTML report could consume 500-2,000 tokens if accidentally read by an agentic session, but the file resides in tests/ which agents do not broadly scan. CI compute overhead for report generation is $0.01-0.05/month. Recommend adding report to .gitignore.
No infrastructure topology or scaling changes. Python 3.9 floor is backward-compatible. pytest-html is lightweight (~50 KB installed) with no native extensions.
